assertions.description: Output includes a labeled character anchor string covering at minimum: apparent age, gender, one distinctive facial feature, hair, and a consistent clothing item
Every scene prompt uses the character anchor string verbatim with no variations in character description
A consistency workflow section explains the --seed and --cref process in plain language

assertion_results.note: Anchor string includes: early 30s, South Asian, wire-rimmed glasses, close-cropped black hair, dark green field jacket
All 4 scene prompts use identical anchor string verbatim
Seed and cref workflow explained in numbered steps
Anchor covers: 50s, grey bob, sharp cheekbones, black turtleneck
Anchor includes: late 20s non-binary, auburn undercut, earring, charcoal overcoat, Moebius aesthetic
All 4 scene prompts use anchor verbatim
Seed/cref workflow explained with v6 specifics

methodology: Binary assertion testing: 3 inputs (standard, minimal, European context) x 3 assertions per skill. Each assertion graded pass/fail based on whether generated output satisfies the requirement.
